title: Operational Reachability Substitution
claim: A self-maintenance workflow is reported operational because its validators, guards, scorer, coder path, verifier path, component tests, and receipts all exist and pass when invoked directly.

observed_evidence.detail: Component-local tests pass when internal functions are invoked directly by the test harness.
Validators, guards, scorer, coder path, and verifier path each produce a passing local result in isolation.

missing_evidence.detail: No runtime trace shows a real operator-style request traversing entrypoint -> FSM -> scorer -> coder -> verifier -> episode.
The declared real entrypoint does not reach the executor: an authorized transition is not wired, so the full chain is not reachable from the real entrypoint.
The required externally observable postcondition has never been produced or independently read back from a real execution.

required_action: Execute the authorized path from the real declared entrypoint through the production router/FSM, required controls, and real executor until the required postcondition occurs and is independently read back; bind input, path, result, and postcondition in a durable episode. Static wiring evidence is not sufficient.
notes: REPRESENTATIVE_EXAMPLE — NOT_A_VERIFIED_HISTORICAL_INCIDENT. No commit id, file path, timestamp, digest, or production receipt is asserted. The defining defect is the substitution of validated local components for an observed working end-to-end system: direct calls to internal components passed, but the real operator entrypoint could not reach the full workflow and the required postcondition was never observed.
